segments.text: You're wasting your money and it's all because of these sneaky design tricks. Here are three design tricks they use. And the last one is more shocking.
Remember in school when you reach the black band of your pencil and thought it was just finished? That was a design trick. It's fake finish line to make you
toss it and buy a new one even when there is still plenty left to write with. That was in school. But now the same trick is in your pocket. Your
phone's battery is designed to get weaker over time. And since it is sealed inside, getting it replaced is expensive and difficult. They make it so
inconvenient that buying a phone feels like a easier option. But printers are the worst. They sell you the printer cheap, but the real cost is the ink. Put
a special chip inside so that you can only buy their super expensive ink. Just no other brand will work. You see,
everything is planned. Subscribe to get smarter with
